public String getHint(String secret, String guess) {

    int bulls = 0;

    int[] secretCount = new int[10];

    int[] guessCount = new int[10];

    for (int i = 0; i < secret.length(); i++) {

        char s = secret.charAt(i);

        char g = guess.charAt(i);

        if (s == g) {

            bulls++;

        } else {

            secretCount[s - '0']++;

            guessCount[g - '0']++;

        }

    }

    int cows = 0;

    for (int i=0; i < 10; i++) {

        cows += Math.min(secretCount[i], guessCount[i]);

    }

    return bulls + "A" + cows + "B";

}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String getHint(String secret, String guess) {
        int bulls = 0;
        int[] secretCount = new int[10];
        int[] guessCount = new int[10];

        for (int i = 0; i < secret.length(); i++) {
            char s = secret.charAt(i);
            char g = guess.charAt(i);
            if (s == g) {
                bulls++;
            } else {
                secretCount[s - '0']++;
                guessCount[g - '0']++;
            }
        }

        int cows = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
            cows += Math.min(secretCount[i], guessCount[i]);
        }

        return bulls + "A" + cows + "B";
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently counts bulls by comparing digits at the same positions. For cows, it uses two fixed-size arrays to track non-bull digit frequencies and sums the minimum counts across all digits. This approach ensures linear time and constant space complexity.
